title: systemd tmpfiles interferes with DirectoryMode in drop-in
body:
### Feature request description

[This](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/167102951749ebe140de76bd049a4df53f437a7c/contrib/tmpfile/podman.conf#L15):

> D! /run/podman 0700 root root

prevents having a different set of permissions and ownership in a DirectoryMode= directive in a drop-in.

### Suggest potential solution

Move this to [podman.socket](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/167102951749ebe140de76bd049a4df53f437a7c/contrib/systemd/system/podman.socket)?

https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/systemd.socket.html#DirectoryMode=

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
@nalcabio-tom thanks for the report, would you like to open a PR for this?

@containers/podman-maintainers wdyt?
@lsm5 not sure if you tagged me on purpose. I really don't know the issue here

> [@lsm5](https://github.com/lsm5) not sure if you tagged me on purpose. I really don't know the issue here

my bad. I had `quadlet` going in my head too.
My main question would be whether we use `/run/podman` for anything besides the socket. If not, I think this is a good (and easy) change.

> My main question would be whether we use `/run/podman` for anything besides the socket.

No clue where these come from, but nv-proxy.socket and nv-proxy.lease are also in there (Debian Trixie).

> If not, I think this is a good (and easy) change.

Thanks everyone for taking the time to respond to this. I'd be happy to submit a pull-request if there's consensus on making this change.

> No clue where these come from, but nv-proxy.socket and nv-proxy.lease are also in there (Debian Trixie).

This is the netavark dhcp proxy:
https://github.com/containers/netavark/blob/main/contrib/systemd/system/netavark-dhcp-proxy.socket

So it should get the same change then.

> I'd be happy to submit a pull-request if there's consensus on making this change.

Please do, I cannot think of a reason why this would not work.

> [@nalcabio-tom](https://github.com/nalcabio-tom) what are you ultimately trying to do?

```
# /etc/systemd/system/podman.socket.d/99nalcabio.conf
[Socket]
DirectoryMode=
DirectoryMode=0755
SocketGroup=
SocketGroup=runner
```

```
# /etc/systemd/system/runner@.service
[Unit]
Description=Runner %i

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/runner-run %i
Type=exec
Restart=always

User=runner
Group=runner
WorkingDirectory=/home/runner
TimeoutSec=0
RestartSec=10

[Install]
WantedBy=runners.target
```

For reasons, this works (minus the socket permissions) as a system-wide service but does not work as a user service.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
